metadata.query_text: VisDrone object detection UAV small objects

items.summary: Object detection in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) images poses significant challenges due to complex scale variations and class imbalance among objects. Existing methods often address these challenges separately, overlooking the intricate nature of UAV images and the potential synergy between them. In response, this paper proposes AD-Det, a novel framework employing a coherent coarse-to-fine strategy that seamlessly integrates two pivotal components: Adaptive Small Object Enhancement (ASOE) and Dynamic Class-balanced Copy-paste (DCC). ASOE utilizes a high-resolution feature map to identify and cluster regions containing small objects. These regions are subsequently enlarged and processed by a fine-grained detector. On the other hand, DCC conducts object-level resampling by dynamically pasting tail classes around the cluster centers obtained by ASOE, main-taining a dynamic memory bank for each tail class. This approach enables AD-Det to not only extract regions with small objects for precise detection but also dynamically perform reasonable resampling for tail-class objects. Consequently, AD-Det enhances the overall detection performance by addressing the challenges of scale variations and class imbalance in UAV images through a synergistic and adaptive framework. We extensively evaluate our approach on two public datasets, i.e., VisDrone and UAVDT, and demonstrate that AD-Det significantly outperforms existing competitive alternatives. Notably, AD-Det achieves a 37.5% Average Precision (AP) on the VisDrone dataset, surpassing its counterparts by at least 3.1%.
items.title: AD-Det: Boosting Object Detection in UAV Images with Focused Small Objects and Balanced Tail Classes

metadata.parent_paper_title: AD-Det: Boosting Object Detection in UAV Images with Focused Small Objects and Balanced Tail Classes

metadata.query_text: VisDrone object detection UAV small objects

items.summary: Object detection is the task of detecting objects in an image. In this task, the detection of small objects is particularly difficult. Other than the small size, it is also accompanied by difficulties due to blur, occlusion, and so on. Current small object detection methods are tailored to small and dense situations, such as pedestrians in a crowd or far objects in remote sensing scenarios. However, when the target object is small and sparse, there is a lack of objects available for training, making it more difficult to learn effective features. In this paper, we propose a specialized method for detecting a specific category of small objects; birds. Particularly, we improve the features learned by the neck; the sub-network between the backbone and the prediction head, to learn more effective features with a hierarchical design. We employ Swin Transformer to upsample the image features. Moreover, we change the shifted window size for adapting to small objects. Experiments show that the proposed Swin Transformer-based neck combined with CenterNet can lead to good performance by changing the window sizes. We further find that smaller window sizes (default 2) benefit mAPs for small object detection.
items.title: Small Object Detection for Birds with Swin Transformer

metadata.query_text: VisDrone object detection UAV small objects

items.summary: Small oriented objects that represent tiny pixel-area in large-scale aerial images are difficult to detect due to their size and orientation. Existing oriented aerial detectors have shown promising results but are mainly focused on orientation modeling with less regard to the size of the objects. In this work, we proposed a method to accurately detect small oriented objects in aerial images by enhancing the classification and regression tasks of the oriented object detection model. We designed the Attention-Points Network consisting of two losses: Guided-Attention Loss (GALoss) and Box-Points Loss (BPLoss). GALoss uses an instance segmentation mask as ground-truth to learn the attention features needed to improve the detection of small objects. These attention features are then used to predict box points for BPLoss, which determines the points' position relative to the target oriented bounding box. Experimental results show the effectiveness of our Attention-Points Network on a standard oriented aerial dataset with small object instances (DOTA-v1.5) and on a maritime-related dataset (HRSC2016). The code is publicly available.
items.title: Improving the Detection of Small Oriented Objects in Aerial Images

metadata.query_text: VisDrone object detection UAV small objects

items.summary: Vehicles, pedestrians, and riders are the most important and interesting objects for the perception modules of self-driving vehicles and video surveillance. However, the state-of-the-art performance of detecting such important objects (esp. small objects) is far from satisfying the demand of practical systems. Large-scale, rich-diversity, and high-resolution datasets play an important role in developing better object detection methods to satisfy the demand. Existing public large-scale datasets such as MS COCO collected from websites do not focus on the specific scenarios. Moreover, the popular datasets (e.g., KITTI and Citypersons) collected from the specific scenarios are limited in the number of images and instances, the resolution, and the diversity. To attempt to solve the problem, we build a diverse high-resolution dataset (called TJU-DHD). The dataset contains 115,354 high-resolution images (52% images have a resolution of 1624$\times$1200 pixels and 48% images have a resolution of at least 2,560$\times$1,440 pixels) and 709,330 labeled objects in total with a large variance in scale and appearance. Meanwhile, the dataset has a rich diversity in season variance, illumination variance, and weather variance. In addition, a new diverse pedestrian dataset is further built. With the four different detectors (i.e., the one-stage RetinaNet, anchor-free FCOS, two-stage FPN, and Cascade R-CNN), experiments about object detection and pedestrian detection are conducted. We hope that the newly built dataset can help promote the research on object detection and pedestrian detection in these two scenes. The dataset is available at https://github.com/tjubiit/TJU-DHD.
items.title: TJU-DHD: A Diverse High-Resolution Dataset for Object Detection

metadata.parent_paper_title: TJU-DHD: A Diverse High-Resolution Dataset for Object Detection

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under 现有小目标检测方法（如AD-Det）在UAV图像上的精度优势已被验证，但其实时性能与能耗指标在轻量化平台上未经评估。, 在特征提取网络（如AD-Det的骨干网络）与检测头之间插入DFS模块，该模块在推理阶段以低成本的方式（如基于阈值的掩码生成）选择性保留高信息量区域，并对低响应区域进行稀疏化处理。DFS模块的开关状态可由用户通过配置文件或运行时参数控制。 should 预期在保持检测精度（AP）基本不变的前提下，推理时的计算成本（如FLOPs）降低15–25%，推理延迟（latency）缩短10–20%，能耗（energy consumption）降低12–18%。 because DFS通过计算特征图的响应强度分布，动态生成二进制掩码，仅保留高响应区域用于后续处理。该机制利用了小目标在特征图中通常表现为局部高响应的特性，通过稀疏化减少冗余计算，从而降低推理时的计算成本。 addresses DFS模块依赖于输入图像的特征响应分布，在极端光照变化（如强逆光或夜间场景）下可能导致特征稀疏化过度，进而影响小目标的检测召回率。, while 设置响应强度阈值的动态调节机制，确保在任何场景下保留的特征区域面积不低于原始特征图的60%，以避免因稀疏化过度导致的精度下降。同时，在DFS模块前后添加特征恢复机制，确保稀疏化处理不会破坏特征的空间连续性。.

integration_contracts.input: 输入为特征提取网络输出的特征图（形状为[B, C, H, W]），其中B为批次大小，C为通道数，H和W为空间尺寸。
integration_contracts.output: 输出为稀疏化后的特征图（形状与输入相同），但低响应区域已被稀疏化处理，仅保留高信息量区域用于后续检测头处理。

hypothesis.condition: 现有小目标检测方法（如AD-Det）在UAV图像上的精度优势已被验证，但其实时性能与能耗指标在轻量化平台上未经评估。
hypothesis.guardrail: 设置响应强度阈值的动态调节机制，确保在任何场景下保留的特征区域面积不低于原始特征图的60%，以避免因稀疏化过度导致的精度下降。同时，在DFS模块前后添加特征恢复机制，确保稀疏化处理不会破坏特征的空间连续性。
hypothesis.intervention: 在特征提取网络（如AD-Det的骨干网络）与检测头之间插入DFS模块，该模块在推理阶段以低成本的方式（如基于阈值的掩码生成）选择性保留高信息量区域，并对低响应区域进行稀疏化处理。DFS模块的开关状态可由用户通过配置文件或运行时参数控制。
hypothesis.limitation: DFS模块依赖于输入图像的特征响应分布，在极端光照变化（如强逆光或夜间场景）下可能导致特征稀疏化过度，进而影响小目标的检测召回率。
hypothesis.mechanism: DFS通过计算特征图的响应强度分布，动态生成二进制掩码，仅保留高响应区域用于后续处理。该机制利用了小目标在特征图中通常表现为局部高响应的特性，通过稀疏化减少冗余计算，从而降低推理时的计算成本。
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: 预期在保持检测精度（AP）基本不变的前提下，推理时的计算成本（如FLOPs）降低15–25%，推理延迟（latency）缩短10–20%，能耗（energy consumption）降低12–18%。

modules.failure_mode: 1) 在特征响应分布极不均匀的场景下，DFS可能过度稀疏化，导致小目标信息丢失，进而降低召回率；2) 动态阈值调节机制失效，导致稀疏化阈值固定过高或过低，影响精度或计算效率。

modules.normalization: inherit and freeze the baseline normalization contract

modules.output_semantics: 输出为稀疏化后的特征图（形状与输入相同），但低响应区域已被稀疏化处理，仅保留高信息量区域用于后续检测头处理。
modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage

research.intended_claim: 引入动态特征稀疏化（Dynamic Feature Sparsification, DFS）模块，该模块在推理阶段根据输入图像的特征响应动态选择性保留高信息量区域，并对低响应区域进行稀疏化处理。DFS作为一个可插拔的前端，与现有检测器（如AD-Det）的特征提取网络无缝集成，仅在目标检测流水线的特征提取阶段进行干预，不对后续检测头或损失函数产生影响。
research.observed_problem: DFS模块依赖于输入图像的特征响应分布，在极端光照变化（如强逆光或夜间场景）下可能导致特征稀疏化过度，进而影响小目标的检测召回率。
research.proposed_mechanism: DFS通过计算特征图的响应强度分布，动态生成二进制掩码，仅保留高响应区域用于后续处理。该机制利用了小目标在特征图中通常表现为局部高响应的特性，通过稀疏化减少冗余计算，从而降低推理时的计算成本。
research.scientific_setting: 聚焦于无人机平台（如MAV、UAV）的实时小目标检测，目标尺寸通常小于图像面积的1%，且受限于嵌入式硬件（如Jetson系列、Raspberry Pi、ESP32-CAM等）的算力与功耗约束。检测对象包括但不限于行人、车辆、动物等，需考虑光照变化、运动模糊、遮挡等野外场景。
research.target_problem: 轻量化无人机小目标检测

supporting_spans: AD-Det: Boosting Object Detection in UAV Images with Focused Small Objects and Balanced Tail Classes.
Object detection in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) images poses significant challenges due to complex scale variations and class imbalance among objects.
Existing methods often address these challenges separately, overlooking the intricate nature of UAV images and the potential synergy between them.
VisDrone.
Dataset 'VisDrone' is explicitly named in the title or abstract of the verified paper 'AD-Det: Boosting Object Detection in UAV Images with Focused Small Objects and Balanced Tail Classes'.

Small Object Detection for Birds with Swin Transformer.
Object detection is the task of detecting objects in an image.
In this task, the detection of small objects is particularly difficult.
Improving the Detection of Small Oriented Objects in Aerial Images.
Small oriented objects that represent tiny pixel-area in large-scale aerial images are difficult to detect due to their size and orientation.
Existing oriented aerial detectors have shown promising results but are mainly focused on orientation modeling with less regard to the size of the objects.
TJU-DHD: A Diverse High-Resolution Dataset for Object Detection.
Vehicles, pedestrians, and riders are the most important and interesting objects for the perception modules of self-driving vehicles and video surveillance.
However, the state-of-the-art performance of detecting such important objects (esp.

metadata.query_text: VisDrone object detection UAV small objects

raw_candidates.snippet: Object detection in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) images poses significant challenges due to complex scale variations and class imbalance among objects. Existing methods often address these challenges separately, overlooking the intricate nature of UAV images and the potential synergy between them. In response, this paper proposes AD-Det, a novel framework employing a coherent coarse-to-fine strategy that seamlessly integrates two pivotal components: Adaptive Small Object Enhancement (ASOE) and Dynamic Class-balanced Copy-paste (DCC). ASOE utilizes a high-resolution feature map to identify and cluster regions containing small objects. These regions are subsequently enlarged and processed by a fine-grained detector. On the other hand, DCC conducts object-level resampling by dynamically pasting tail classes around the cluster centers obtained by ASOE, main-taining a dynamic memory bank for each tail class. This approach enables AD-Det to not only extract regions with small objects for precise detection but also dynamically perform reasonable resampling for tail-class objects. Consequently, AD-Det enhances the overall detection performance by addressing the challenges of scale variations and class imbalance in UAV images through a synergistic and adaptive framework. We extensively evaluate our approach on two public datasets, i.e., VisDrone and UAVDT, and demonstrate that AD-Det significantly outperforms existing competitive alternatives. Notably, AD-Det achieves a 37.5% Average Precision (AP) on the VisDrone dataset, surpassing its counterparts by at least 3.1%.
raw_candidates.title: AD-Det: Boosting Object Detection in UAV Images with Focused Small Objects and Balanced Tail Classes

metadata.query_text: VisDrone object detection UAV small objects

raw_candidates.snippet: Object detection is the task of detecting objects in an image. In this task, the detection of small objects is particularly difficult. Other than the small size, it is also accompanied by difficulties due to blur, occlusion, and so on. Current small object detection methods are tailored to small and dense situations, such as pedestrians in a crowd or far objects in remote sensing scenarios. However, when the target object is small and sparse, there is a lack of objects available for training, making it more difficult to learn effective features. In this paper, we propose a specialized method for detecting a specific category of small objects; birds. Particularly, we improve the features learned by the neck; the sub-network between the backbone and the prediction head, to learn more effective features with a hierarchical design. We employ Swin Transformer to upsample the image features. Moreover, we change the shifted window size for adapting to small objects. Experiments show that the proposed Swin Transformer-based neck combined with CenterNet can lead to good performance by changing the window sizes. We further find that smaller window sizes (default 2) benefit mAPs for small object detection.
raw_candidates.title: Small Object Detection for Birds with Swin Transformer

metadata.query_text: VisDrone object detection UAV small objects

raw_candidates.snippet: Small oriented objects that represent tiny pixel-area in large-scale aerial images are difficult to detect due to their size and orientation. Existing oriented aerial detectors have shown promising results but are mainly focused on orientation modeling with less regard to the size of the objects. In this work, we proposed a method to accurately detect small oriented objects in aerial images by enhancing the classification and regression tasks of the oriented object detection model. We designed the Attention-Points Network consisting of two losses: Guided-Attention Loss (GALoss) and Box-Points Loss (BPLoss). GALoss uses an instance segmentation mask as ground-truth to learn the attention features needed to improve the detection of small objects. These attention features are then used to predict box points for BPLoss, which determines the points' position relative to the target oriented bounding box. Experimental results show the effectiveness of our Attention-Points Network on a standard oriented aerial dataset with small object instances (DOTA-v1.5) and on a maritime-related dataset (HRSC2016). The code is publicly available.
raw_candidates.title: Improving the Detection of Small Oriented Objects in Aerial Images

metadata.query_text: VisDrone object detection UAV small objects

raw_candidates.snippet: Vehicles, pedestrians, and riders are the most important and interesting objects for the perception modules of self-driving vehicles and video surveillance. However, the state-of-the-art performance of detecting such important objects (esp. small objects) is far from satisfying the demand of practical systems. Large-scale, rich-diversity, and high-resolution datasets play an important role in developing better object detection methods to satisfy the demand. Existing public large-scale datasets such as MS COCO collected from websites do not focus on the specific scenarios. Moreover, the popular datasets (e.g., KITTI and Citypersons) collected from the specific scenarios are limited in the number of images and instances, the resolution, and the diversity. To attempt to solve the problem, we build a diverse high-resolution dataset (called TJU-DHD). The dataset contains 115,354 high-resolution images (52% images have a resolution of 1624$\times$1200 pixels and 48% images have a resolution of at least 2,560$\times$1,440 pixels) and 709,330 labeled objects in total with a large variance in scale and appearance. Meanwhile, the dataset has a rich diversity in season variance, illumination variance, and weather variance. In addition, a new diverse pedestrian dataset is further built. With the four different detectors (i.e., the one-stage RetinaNet, anchor-free FCOS, two-stage FPN, and Cascade R-CNN), experiments about object detection and pedestrian detection are conducted. We hope that the newly built dataset can help promote the research on object detection and pedestrian detection in these two scenes. The dataset is available at https://github.com/tjubiit/TJU-DHD.
raw_candidates.title: TJU-DHD: A Diverse High-Resolution Dataset for Object Detection

items.summary: In this paper, a deep neural network based ensemble method is experimented for automatic identification of skin disease from dermoscopic images. The developed algorithm is applied on the task3 of the ISIC 2018 challenge dataset (Skin Lesion Analysis Towards Melanoma Detection).
items.title: Skin disease identification from dermoscopy images using deep convolutional neural network

metadata.query_text: skin lesion dataset clinical photography public benchmark

items.summary: Over the past few years, different Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems have been proposed to tackle skin lesion analysis. Most of these systems work only for dermoscopy images since there is a strong lack of public clinical images archive available to evaluate the aforementioned CAD systems. To fill this gap, we release a skin lesion benchmark composed of clinical images collected from smartphone devices and a set of patient clinical data containing up to 21 features. The dataset consists of 1373 patients, 1641 skin lesions, and 2298 images for six different diagnostics: three skin diseases and three skin cancers. In total, 58.4% of the skin lesions are biopsy-proven, including 100% of the skin cancers. By releasing this benchmark, we aim to support future research and the development of new tools to assist clinicians to detect skin cancer.
items.title: PAD-UFES-20: A skin lesion dataset composed of patient data and clinical images collected from smartphones

metadata.query_text: skin lesion dataset clinical photography public benchmark

items.summary:
BACKGROUND
While dermatoscopy's added value to clinical imaging is known, the standalone value of dermatoscopy and clinical images remains unclear.


OBJECTIVE
To quantify the standalone and complementary value of dermatoscopy and clinical close-up imaging across lesion types and reader expertise.


METHODS
In an online reader study, 283 participants diagnosed 1567 paired images (clinical close-up/dermatoscopic) of skin cancers and mimics. The presentation order was randomized. Diagnostic accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity were compared. The effects of modality, reader expertise, and presentation order were analyzed using a generalized linear mixed model.


RESULTS
Dermatoscopy alone showed higher sensitivity (85.0% vs 74.2%) but lower specificity (66.8% vs 71.9%) compared with clinical close-up images alone. It improved accuracy for melanoma and basal cell carcinoma, but not for nevi. Adding either modality increased the odds of a correct diagnosis. Dermatoscopy raised the odds by 52% (OR = 1.52; 95% CI: 1.36-1.70; P < .001), while clinical close-ups increased the odds by 40% (OR = 1.40; 95% CI: 1.20-1.61; P < .001).


LIMITATIONS
Image-based evaluation simulated teledermatology rather than face-to-face assessment; potential selection and verification bias cannot be excluded.


CONCLUSION
Dermatoscopy alone yields higher sensitivity for malignant lesions but lower specificity for nevi than clinical close-up images. The latter provides complementary diagnostic cues, underscoring the value of integrating both modalities for optimal assessment.
items.title: Comparative and complementary diagnostic value of dermatoscopy and clinical close-up photography in skin cancer diagnosis: A study from the MILK10k dataset.

items.summary: Accurate diagnosis of skin lesions remains challenging due to their morphological variability and the limitations of conventional diagnostic methods. In this study, we developed an explainable artificial intelligence (AI) framework that integrates three-dimensional total body photography (3D TBP) images with structured clinical data to classify and assess the risk of six common skin-lesion types. Using the ISIC 2024 dataset comprising 1,075 patients, 41 clinical and lesion-specific features were extracted and analyzed. A multinomial logistic-regression model was implemented for decision support, and model interpretability was assessed using Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) and Class Activation Maps (CAM). The clinical-only XGBoost model achieved moderate accuracy (basal cell carcinoma 78.6%, nevus 72.6%), while CNNs trained on 3D TBP images achieved 87.1% accuracy for nevus. The multimodal fusion model substantially outperformed unimodal approaches, achieving recall and F1 scores above 95% and Area Under the Curve (AUC) values exceeding 0.95 (0.98 for nevus and actinic keratosis), and ranked among the top-performing entries in the ISIC 2024 challenge (partial false-positive rate = 0.1734). The integrated scoring system, visualized through nomograms, identified key predictors such as visual_classifier and tbp_lv_symm_2axis. This interpretable multimodal AI framework enhances diagnostic accuracy and risk stratification, offering a transparent and clinically actionable tool for precision dermatology and early detection of skin cancer.
items.title: Explainable multimodal AI for skin lesion risk prediction via 3D imaging and clinical data

metadata.query_text: skin lesion dataset clinical photography public benchmark

items.summary: Early detection of cutaneous melanoma is crucial for improving patient survival rates. Skin lesion segmentation is a critical step towards computer-aided diagnosis that is effective in melanoma detection. This work proposes a novel patch-based model that leverages a combination of non-negative matrix factorization and mutual information for segmenting cutaneous melanomas in clinical images. The proposed approach tends to lead to accurate skin lesion segmentations, and it was comprehensively evaluated in terms of its effectiveness based on three benchmark datasets (DermIS, DermNet, and DermQuest) using statistical analysis, ablation studies, and cross-dataset testing. The proposed method tends to improve on the segmentation results provided by established deep learning baselines (FCNet, FCN, PSPNet, SegNet, U-Net) and by recent advancements specifically designed for skin lesion segmentation (DSNet, FrCNet, and LGPNet).
items.title: Skin Lesion Segmentation in Clinical Images Using Nonnegative Matrix Factorization and Mutual Information

metadata.query_text: skin lesion dataset clinical photography public benchmark

items.summary: The rising incidence of skin cancer necessitates the development of automated, reliable diagnostic tools to support clinicians. While deep learning models, particularly from the YOLO family, have shown promise, their application in real-world scenarios is limited by challenges such as class imbalance and the inability to process images of healthy skin, leading to potential false positives. This study presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of 32 object detection models, primarily from the YOLO architecture (v5–v12) and RT-DETR, to identify the most effective solution for skin lesion detection. We curated a novel, balanced dataset of 10,000 images based on the ISIC archive, comprising 10 distinct lesion classes (benign and malignant). Crucially, we introduced a dedicated ‘background’ class containing 1000 images of clear skin, a novelty designed to enhance model robustness in clinical practice. Models were systematically evaluated and filtered based on performance metrics (mAP, Recall) and complexity. Through a multi-stage evaluation, the YOLOv9c model was identified as the superior architecture, achieving a mAP@50 of 72.5% and a Recall of 71.6% across all classes. The model demonstrated strong performance, especially considering the dataset’s complexity with 10 classes and background images. Our research establishes a new benchmark for skin lesion detection. We demonstrate that including a ‘background’ class is a critical step towards creating clinically viable tools. The YOLOv9c model emerges as a powerful and efficient solution. To foster further research, our curated 10-class dataset with background images will be made publicly available.
items.title: Enchancing Robustness in Skin Lesion Detection: A Benchmark of 32 Models on a Novel Dataset Including Healthy Skin Images

ablations.expected_observation: recover the unchanged frozen baseline path
ablations.name: module-off parity

ablations.change: enable only lesion_type_calibrator under the matched budget
ablations.expected_observation: Improved clinical utility by reducing false positives for nevi (a common benign lesion) while maintaining high sensitivity for malignant lesions, aligning with clinician needs for specificity in low-risk cases.
ablations.name: single-module causal test

baseline.description: Accepted evidence constrains baseline selection; implementation version, reproduction, and deployment fit remain pilot gates.
baseline.name: unresolved task-matched baseline; retrieve and reproduce an evidence-bound comparator

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under Baseline models (e.g., YOLOv9c, CNN ensembles) are already trained and evaluated on public datasets (e.g., ISIC archives) with reported metrics (mAP, recall, AUC)., Add a switchable calibration module that applies lesion-type-specific temperature scaling to the base model's logits. The module is trained on a held-out validation set (e.g., 20% of ISIC 2018) and applied only during inference, ensuring no change to the base model's weights or training pipeline. should Expected reduction in false positive rate for nevi by 15–25% while maintaining or slightly improving sensitivity for malignant lesions (e.g., melanoma, BCC). Primary metric: F1-score for nevi; secondary metric: sensitivity for malignant lesions. because Failure mechanism: Overconfident predictions for nevi lead to false positives. Mechanism: Post-hoc calibration adjusts confidence scores using a lesion-type-specific temperature scaling derived from SHAP-weighted validation samples, reducing overconfidence without retraining the base model. addresses Calibration may reduce performance on rare lesion types due to limited validation samples; requires careful tuning to avoid overfitting to specific datasets., while Calibration module must not degrade performance on malignant lesions (e.g., sensitivity < 80% for melanoma) or increase false negatives for high-risk classes. Stop if calibration worsens AUC for any lesion type by > 2%..

integration_contracts.input: Input: Base model logits (e.g., YOLOv9c or CNN ensemble outputs) for a batch of dermoscopic or clinical images, paired with lesion-type labels (e.g., nevus, melanoma, BCC). Input must include a held-out validation set for calibration training.
integration_contracts.output: Output: Calibrated confidence scores per lesion type, ensuring reduced overconfidence for nevi and maintained or improved sensitivity for malignant lesions. Output is a tensor of adjusted probabilities matching the input logits' shape.

baseline.dataset: unresolved task-matched data source; no public dataset is required at discovery time; use task-appropriate user-owned, synthetic, simulated, or newly collected data and freeze provenance, split, license, and fingerprint before execution
baseline.environment: not yet frozen; record hardware, framework, precision, export path, and dependency lock
baseline.name: unresolved task-matched baseline; retrieve and reproduce an evidence-bound comparator
baseline.seed_policy: three fixed seeds (1, 2, 3) for all pilot comparisons
baseline.split: not yet frozen; preserve the documented benchmark split and record data hashes
baseline.version_or_commit: baseline identity unresolved; retrieve an evidence-bound comparator and freeze its implementation before integration

limitations: Limited generalizability to underrepresented lesion types or populations in public datasets.
Potential selection bias in curated datasets (e.g., ISIC subsets).
Performance variability across lesion types and datasets (e.g., lower specificity for nevi in dermatoscopy).
Class imbalance and false positives remain challenges in real-world deployment.

Limited standardization of evaluation protocols across studies.
supported_claims: A deep convolutional neural network ensemble method achieved competitive performance on the ISIC 2018 challenge dataset for skin lesion classification.
evidence.title: Skin disease identification from dermoscopy images using deep convolutional neural network

supported_claims: Dermatoscopy alone shows higher sensitivity (85.0%) but lower specificity (66.8%) for skin lesion diagnosis compared to clinical close-up images (sensitivity 74.2%, specificity 71.9%).

Adding either dermatoscopy or clinical close-up imaging increases the odds of a correct diagnosis, with dermatoscopy raising the odds by 52% (OR = 1.52; 95% CI: 1.36–1.70; P < .001) and clinical close-ups by 40% (OR = 1.40; 95% CI: 1.20–1.61; P < .001).
evidence.title: Comparative and complementary diagnostic value of dermatoscopy and clinical close-up photography in skin cancer diagnosis: A study from the MILK10k dataset.

supported_claims: Multimodal AI frameworks integrating 3D total body photography (3D TBP) with clinical data achieve high diagnostic accuracy (e.g., 87.1% for nevus) and AUC values exceeding 0.95 for multiple lesion types.
Explainable AI methods such as SHAP and Class Activation Maps (CAM) are used to enhance interpretability of skin lesion classification models.
evidence.title: Explainable multimodal AI for skin lesion risk prediction via 3D imaging and clinical data

supported_claims: Including a dedicated ‘background’ class (images of clear skin) in training datasets improves model robustness and reduces false positives in skin lesion detection.
The YOLOv9c model achieved a mAP@50 of 72.5% and a Recall of 71.6% across 10 lesion classes in a curated dataset including background images.

hypothesis.condition: Baseline models (e.g., YOLOv9c, CNN ensembles) are already trained and evaluated on public datasets (e.g., ISIC archives) with reported metrics (mAP, recall, AUC).
hypothesis.guardrail: Calibration module must not degrade performance on malignant lesions (e.g., sensitivity < 80% for melanoma) or increase false negatives for high-risk classes. Stop if calibration worsens AUC for any lesion type by > 2%.
hypothesis.intervention: Add a switchable calibration module that applies lesion-type-specific temperature scaling to the base model's logits. The module is trained on a held-out validation set (e.g., 20% of ISIC 2018) and applied only during inference, ensuring no change to the base model's weights or training pipeline.
hypothesis.limitation: Calibration may reduce performance on rare lesion types due to limited validation samples; requires careful tuning to avoid overfitting to specific datasets.
hypothesis.mechanism: Failure mechanism: Overconfident predictions for nevi lead to false positives. Mechanism: Post-hoc calibration adjusts confidence scores using a lesion-type-specific temperature scaling derived from SHAP-weighted validation samples, reducing overconfidence without retraining the base model.
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: Expected reduction in false positive rate for nevi by 15–25% while maintaining or slightly improving sensitivity for malignant lesions (e.g., melanoma, BCC). Primary metric: F1-score for nevi; secondary metric: sensitivity for malignant lesions.

method.problem_method_insight: Dermatoscopy and clinical close-up imaging provide complementary diagnostic cues, with dermatoscopy improving sensitivity for malignant lesions but lowering specificity for nevi. Multimodal fusion (e.g., 3D total body photography + clinical data) achieves high accuracy and AUC, outperforming unimodal approaches. However, class imbalance and false positives remain challenges in real-world deployment, particularly for underrepresented lesion types or populations.

supporting_spans: Skin disease identification from dermoscopy images using deep convolutional neural network.
In this paper, a deep neural network based ensemble method is experimented for automatic identification of skin disease from dermoscopic images.
The developed algorithm is applied on the task3 of the ISIC 2018 challenge dataset (Skin Lesion Analysis Towards Melanoma Detection).
PAD-UFES-20: A skin lesion dataset composed of patient data and clinical images collected from smartphones.
Over the past few years, different Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems have been proposed to tackle skin lesion analysis.
Most of these systems work only for dermoscopy images since there is a strong lack of public clinical images archive available to evaluate the aforementioned CAD systems.
Comparative and complementary diagnostic value of dermatoscopy and clinical close-up photography in skin cancer diagnosis: A study from the MILK10k dataset..

BACKGROUND
While dermatoscopy's added value to clinical imaging is known, the standalone value of dermatoscopy and clinical images remains unclear.

OBJECTIVE
To quantify the standalone and complementary value of dermatoscopy and clinical close-up imaging across lesion types and reader expertise.

Dataset 'MILK10k' is explicitly named in the title or abstract of the verified paper 'Comparative and complementary diagnostic value of dermatoscopy and clinical close-up photography in skin cancer diagnosis: A study from the MILK10k dataset.'.
This verifies the dataset mention, not an official download page or split manifest.
Explainable multimodal AI for skin lesion risk prediction via 3D imaging and clinical data.
Accurate diagnosis of skin lesions remains challenging due to their morphological variability and the limitations of conventional diagnostic methods.

In this study, we developed an explainable artificial intelligence (AI) framework that integrates three-dimensional total body photography (3D TBP) images with structured clinical data to classify and assess the risk of six common skin-lesion types.
Skin Lesion Segmentation in Clinical Images Using Nonnegative Matrix Factorization and Mutual Information.
Skin lesion segmentation is a critical step towards computer-aided diagnosis that is effective in melanoma detection.
This work proposes a novel patch-based model that leverages a combination of non-negative matrix factorization and mutual information for segmenting cutaneous melanomas in clinical images.
Enchancing Robustness in Skin Lesion Detection: A Benchmark of 32 Models on a Novel Dataset Including Healthy Skin Images.
The rising incidence of skin cancer necessitates the development of automated, reliable diagnostic tools to support clinicians.

While deep learning models, particularly from the YOLO family, have shown promise, their application in real-world scenarios is limited by challenges such as class imbalance and the inability to process images of healthy skin, leading to potential false positives.

metadata.query_text: skin lesion dataset clinical photography public benchmark

raw_candidates.snippet: Over the past few years, different Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems have been proposed to tackle skin lesion analysis. Most of these systems work only for dermoscopy images since there is a strong lack of public clinical images archive available to evaluate the aforementioned CAD systems. To fill this gap, we release a skin lesion benchmark composed of clinical images collected from smartphone devices and a set of patient clinical data containing up to 21 features. The dataset consists of 1373 patients, 1641 skin lesions, and 2298 images for six different diagnostics: three skin diseases and three skin cancers. In total, 58.4% of the skin lesions are biopsy-proven, including 100% of the skin cancers. By releasing this benchmark, we aim to support future research and the development of new tools to assist clinicians to detect skin cancer.
raw_candidates.title: PAD-UFES-20: A skin lesion dataset composed of patient data and clinical images collected from smartphones

metadata.query_text: skin lesion dataset clinical photography public benchmark

raw_candidates.snippet:
BACKGROUND
While dermatoscopy's added value to clinical imaging is known, the standalone value of dermatoscopy and clinical images remains unclear.


OBJECTIVE
To quantify the standalone and complementary value of dermatoscopy and clinical close-up imaging across lesion types and reader expertise.


METHODS
In an online reader study, 283 participants diagnosed 1567 paired images (clinical close-up/dermatoscopic) of skin cancers and mimics. The presentation order was randomized. Diagnostic accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity were compared. The effects of modality, reader expertise, and presentation order were analyzed using a generalized linear mixed model.


RESULTS
Dermatoscopy alone showed higher sensitivity (85.0% vs 74.2%) but lower specificity (66.8% vs 71.9%) compared with clinical close-up images alone. It improved accuracy for melanoma and basal cell carcinoma, but not for nevi. Adding either modality increased the odds of a correct diagnosis. Dermatoscopy raised the odds by 52% (OR = 1.52; 95% CI: 1.36-1.70; P < .001), while clinical close-ups increased the odds by 40% (OR = 1.40; 95% CI: 1.20-1.61; P < .001).


LIMITATIONS
Image-based evaluation simulated teledermatology rather than face-to-face assessment; potential selection and verification bias cannot be excluded.


CONCLUSION
Dermatoscopy alone yields higher sensitivity for malignant lesions but lower specificity for nevi than clinical close-up images. The latter provides complementary diagnostic cues, underscoring the value of integrating both modalities for optimal assessment.

raw_candidates.snippet: Accurate diagnosis of skin lesions remains challenging due to their morphological variability and the limitations of conventional diagnostic methods. In this study, we developed an explainable artificial intelligence (AI) framework that integrates three-dimensional total body photography (3D TBP) images with structured clinical data to classify and assess the risk of six common skin-lesion types. Using the ISIC 2024 dataset comprising 1,075 patients, 41 clinical and lesion-specific features were extracted and analyzed. A multinomial logistic-regression model was implemented for decision support, and model interpretability was assessed using Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) and Class Activation Maps (CAM). The clinical-only XGBoost model achieved moderate accuracy (basal cell carcinoma 78.6%, nevus 72.6%), while CNNs trained on 3D TBP images achieved 87.1% accuracy for nevus. The multimodal fusion model substantially outperformed unimodal approaches, achieving recall and F1 scores above 95% and Area Under the Curve (AUC) values exceeding 0.95 (0.98 for nevus and actinic keratosis), and ranked among the top-performing entries in the ISIC 2024 challenge (partial false-positive rate = 0.1734). The integrated scoring system, visualized through nomograms, identified key predictors such as visual_classifier and tbp_lv_symm_2axis. This interpretable multimodal AI framework enhances diagnostic accuracy and risk stratification, offering a transparent and clinically actionable tool for precision dermatology and early detection of skin cancer.
raw_candidates.title: Explainable multimodal AI for skin lesion risk prediction via 3D imaging and clinical data

metadata.query_text: skin lesion dataset clinical photography public benchmark

raw_candidates.snippet: Early detection of cutaneous melanoma is crucial for improving patient survival rates. Skin lesion segmentation is a critical step towards computer-aided diagnosis that is effective in melanoma detection. This work proposes a novel patch-based model that leverages a combination of non-negative matrix factorization and mutual information for segmenting cutaneous melanomas in clinical images. The proposed approach tends to lead to accurate skin lesion segmentations, and it was comprehensively evaluated in terms of its effectiveness based on three benchmark datasets (DermIS, DermNet, and DermQuest) using statistical analysis, ablation studies, and cross-dataset testing. The proposed method tends to improve on the segmentation results provided by established deep learning baselines (FCNet, FCN, PSPNet, SegNet, U-Net) and by recent advancements specifically designed for skin lesion segmentation (DSNet, FrCNet, and LGPNet).
raw_candidates.title: Skin Lesion Segmentation in Clinical Images Using Nonnegative Matrix Factorization and Mutual Information

metadata.query_text: skin lesion dataset clinical photography public benchmark

raw_candidates.snippet: The rising incidence of skin cancer necessitates the development of automated, reliable diagnostic tools to support clinicians. While deep learning models, particularly from the YOLO family, have shown promise, their application in real-world scenarios is limited by challenges such as class imbalance and the inability to process images of healthy skin, leading to potential false positives. This study presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of 32 object detection models, primarily from the YOLO architecture (v5–v12) and RT-DETR, to identify the most effective solution for skin lesion detection. We curated a novel, balanced dataset of 10,000 images based on the ISIC archive, comprising 10 distinct lesion classes (benign and malignant). Crucially, we introduced a dedicated ‘background’ class containing 1000 images of clear skin, a novelty designed to enhance model robustness in clinical practice. Models were systematically evaluated and filtered based on performance metrics (mAP, Recall) and complexity. Through a multi-stage evaluation, the YOLOv9c model was identified as the superior architecture, achieving a mAP@50 of 72.5% and a Recall of 71.6% across all classes. The model demonstrated strong performance, especially considering the dataset’s complexity with 10 classes and background images. Our research establishes a new benchmark for skin lesion detection. We demonstrate that including a ‘background’ class is a critical step towards creating clinically viable tools. The YOLOv9c model emerges as a powerful and efficient solution. To foster further research, our curated 10-class dataset with background images will be made publicly available.
raw_candidates.title: Enchancing Robustness in Skin Lesion Detection: A Benchmark of 32 Models on a Novel Dataset Including Healthy Skin Images

verified_findings.text: Dermatoscopy alone shows higher sensitivity (85.0%) but lower specificity (66.8%) for skin lesion diagnosis compared to clinical close-up images (sensitivity 74.2%, specificity 71.9%).

Adding either dermatoscopy or clinical close-up imaging increases the odds of a correct diagnosis, with dermatoscopy raising the odds by 52% (OR = 1.52; 95% CI: 1.36–1.70; P < .001) and clinical close-ups by 40% (OR = 1.40; 95% CI: 1.20–1.61; P < .001).
A deep convolutional neural network ensemble method achieved competitive performance on the ISIC 2018 challenge dataset for skin lesion classification.
Including a dedicated ‘background’ class (images of clear skin) in training datasets improves model robustness and reduces false positives in skin lesion detection.

Multimodal AI frameworks integrating 3D total body photography (3D TBP) with clinical data achieve high diagnostic accuracy (e.g., 87.1% for nevus) and AUC values exceeding 0.95 for multiple lesion types.
Explainable AI methods such as SHAP and Class Activation Maps (CAM) are used to enhance interpretability of skin lesion classification models.
The YOLOv9c model achieved a mAP@50 of 72.5% and a Recall of 71.6% across 10 lesion classes in a curated dataset including background images.

metadata.query_text: steel surface defect detection deep learning NEU-DET RSDD GC10-DET SEVERSTAL

items.summary: Steel surface defect detection plays a critical quality control role in industrial manufacturing. However, the existing methods struggle to balance accuracy and efficiency, especially in complex defect environments, where significant challenges persist. To address this challenge, FD-YOLO11, which is a YOLO11-based deep learning model with enhanced feature extraction and fusion mechanisms for attaining improved detection performance, is proposed in this paper. To enhance the multiscale feature extraction process, self-calibrated convolution is integrated into the C3k2 module. Additionally, an FSPPF structure is designed to optimize the process of fusing local and global information, improving the defect recognition ability of the model in complex backgrounds. Furthermore, the DySample mechanism replaces the traditional upsampling method, effectively refining the feature fusion process and minimizing the semantic information loss. The NEU-DET and GC10-DET datasets are used for evaluation purposes, and the experimental results demonstrate that FD-YOLO11 achieves 4.6% and 4.0% mAP@0.5 improvements over YOLO11s while maintaining an inference speed comparable to that of YOLO11s. This ensures a compromise between detection performance and computational efficiency. The model exhibits enhanced recognition capabilities and greater robustness in complex defect detection tasks. This research indicates that FD-YOLO11 provides a high-precision solution for metal surface defect detection, with broad application potential in intelligent manufacturing and industrial inspection scenarios.
items.title: FD-YOLO11: A Feature-Enhanced Deep Learning Model for Steel Surface Defect Detection

items.summary: With the development of industrial automation, steel surface defect detection is very important to improve production quality. Traditional methods are limited by efficiency and accuracy, but deep learning technology provides new ideas for this. In this paper, a steel surface defect detection algorithm (YOLO-GH) based on improved YOLO11 is proposed. By replacing C3k2 module in backbone network with C3k2_HFERB module, high-frequency feature capture capability is enhanced. A global-local self-attention module (GLSA) is introduced into the neck network to optimize multi-scale feature fusion. The experiment was based on NEU-DET data set, and the results showed that the mAP50 of the improved model reached 81.4%, which was 5.5% higher than that of the basic model, the parameter number was reduced to 2.52M, and the computation amount was only 6.1 GFLOPs, which was significantly better than the mainstream models such as YOLOv7 and YOLOv8. The algorithm achieves a balance between precision and efficiency, and provides an efficient solution for industrial real-time inspection.
items.title: Steel surface defect detection algorithm based on improved YOLO11

metadata.query_text: steel surface defect detection deep learning NEU-DET RSDD GC10-DET SEVERSTAL

items.summary: Given that Steel surface defects generally exhibit characteristics such as small size, edge blur, irregular shape, complex background page, and variable light, Traditional detection methods and existing Deep learning models still have deficiencies in terms of Detection accuracy, Robustness, and Real-time performance. To address this, this paper proposes an optimization-oriented Lightweight surface defect detection model for Industrial grayscale images based on the RT-DETR framework. By introducing three Improved modules: SNI-FCA, CGFM, and GSConvE-GACW, the model's Perception ability for Subtle defects is enhanced through three key aspects: Feature reconstruction, Multi-scale fusion, and Input representation. The experiment results on the NEU-DET dataset show that the proposed method achieves a Mean Average Precision of 77.43 % without relying on a Bigger Backbone network, which is significantly superior to Mainstream models such as YOLOv5l, YOLOv8I, and YOLOv11I, achieving significant improvement in recall rate compared to other methods. Meanwhile, the Model parameters are $\mathbf{1 9. 7 2 M}$, and the Computational complexity is 56.4 Giga Floating - Point Operations Per Second, meeting the industrial Real-time performance requirement. Ablation experiment verifies the effectiveness and Synergistic gain of each module. This method achieves a good Balance among accuracy, velocity, and Generalization ability, providing an efficient and robust solution for Automated surface quality inspection in Industrial realms such as steel.
items.title: Steel Surface Defect Detection Algorithm Based on Improved RT-DETR

items.summary: ABSTRACT Thin strip steel, as a primary product in the metallurgical industry, is extensively utilised across various fields, so it is crucial to carry out non-destructive testing of its defects during production. Existing datasets, such as NEU-DET, are incomplete and the contrast of images differs from the actual production. Therefore, combined with NEU-DET, a new dataset is constructed by adding self-built hole and edge crack defect images. Additionally, a defect detection algorithm based on image enhancement and deep learning was proposed. The PSO-Gabor algorithm enhanced the contrast of defect images and highlighted the defect features. An optimised Faster RCNN network was proposed, which used the improved AlexNet as the feature extraction network. The SE attention and the depthwise convolution were introduced to capture local features.an IoU K-means improved the accuracy of anchor box generation. The pooling layer was replaced to address the feature maps size deviations. Finally, a defect detection platform was designed to conduct an experimental validation. The results showed that the improved AlexNet network achieved a classification accuracy of 99.78% for surface defects on steel plates, which was a 17.9% improvement over that of traditional networks. The optimised Faster RCNN achieved a mAP improvement of 0.14 while maintaining an FPS of 35.
items.title: Steel surface defect detection based on PSO-Gabor and an improved faster RCNN

metadata.query_text: steel surface defect detection deep learning NEU-DET RSDD GC10-DET SEVERSTAL

items.summary: Accurate and efficient detection of small and complex surface defects on hot-rolled steel plates remains a significant challenge in industrial quality assurance. Current deep learning detectors often exhibit limitations in detection accuracy and training convergence speed, particularly for small objects, which limits their practical deployment in real-time industrial inspection systems. To overcome these deficiencies, this paper proposes a multi-scale deformable transformer iterative query refinement network (MDT-Net). MDT integrates three key innovations: a Swin Transformer backbone for robust multi-scale feature representation, a deformable attention mechanism to significantly reduce computational complexity and accelerate convergence, and an iterative bounding box refinement strategy for precise localization. Extensive experiments on the NEU-DET dataset demonstrate MDT’s superior performance, achieving 82.7% mAP50. Crucially, MDT significantly outperforms other mainstream detectors in small object detection, reaching an mAP50:95 of 0.55, and exhibits remarkably faster training convergence. These findings confirm MDT’s effectiveness and robustness for accurate and efficient steel surface defect detection, thereby providing a crucial tool for enhancing sensor-based quality control and offering a promising solution for industrial quality management.
items.title: Multi-Scale Deformable Transformer with Iterative Query Refinement for Hot-Rolled Steel Surface Defect Detection

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under 现有方法在NEU-DET与GC10-DET数据集上已实现基线性能，但小目标检测精度（mAP50:95）仍有提升空间。, 在YOLO11s骨干网络的P3、P4、P5特征层中插入MSFAM模块，替换原有的上采样与特征融合机制（如FSPPF）。该干预仅涉及特征融合层的替换，无需修改骨干网络或检测头，可独立开关。 should 预期在NEU-DET与GC10-DET数据集上，mAP@0.5提升2.0–3.5%，mAP50:95提升4.0–6.0%，同时保持推理延迟增加<15%（相对于YOLO11s基线）。 because MSFAM通过分层自适应注意力机制动态聚合多尺度特征，并引入可变形卷积增强小目标边界感知。具体包括：(1) 多尺度特征分组与自适应权重分配；(2) 可变形卷积增强局部特征提取；(3) 轻量级注意力门控机制优化特征融合。该机制独立于骨干网络，可与现有YOLO或Transformer架构兼容。 addresses MSFAM的有效性依赖于多尺度特征的充分表达，在极端小目标（<10像素）或严重遮挡场景下可能受限。此外，模块参数开销（约0.8M）需在实时性与精度间进行权衡。, while 为避免过拟合，MSFAM模块需在训练时采用DropPath（p=0.2）与特征归一化（L2）约束。此外，需监控小目标召回率（IoU<0.3）的变化，若下降>5%，则回滚至基线配置。.

hypothesis.condition: 现有方法在NEU-DET与GC10-DET数据集上已实现基线性能，但小目标检测精度（mAP50:95）仍有提升空间。
hypothesis.guardrail: 为避免过拟合，MSFAM模块需在训练时采用DropPath（p=0.2）与特征归一化（L2）约束。此外，需监控小目标召回率（IoU<0.3）的变化，若下降>5%，则回滚至基线配置。
hypothesis.intervention: 在YOLO11s骨干网络的P3、P4、P5特征层中插入MSFAM模块，替换原有的上采样与特征融合机制（如FSPPF）。该干预仅涉及特征融合层的替换，无需修改骨干网络或检测头，可独立开关。
hypothesis.limitation: MSFAM的有效性依赖于多尺度特征的充分表达，在极端小目标（<10像素）或严重遮挡场景下可能受限。此外，模块参数开销（约0.8M）需在实时性与精度间进行权衡。
hypothesis.mechanism: MSFAM通过分层自适应注意力机制动态聚合多尺度特征，并引入可变形卷积增强小目标边界感知。具体包括：(1) 多尺度特征分组与自适应权重分配；(2) 可变形卷积增强局部特征提取；(3) 轻量级注意力门控机制优化特征融合。该机制独立于骨干网络，可与现有YOLO或Transformer架构兼容。
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: 预期在NEU-DET与GC10-DET数据集上，mAP@0.5提升2.0–3.5%，mAP50:95提升4.0–6.0%，同时保持推理延迟增加<15%（相对于YOLO11s基线）。

assumptions: 钢材表面缺陷类型与数据集分布可能存在显著差异，导致跨数据集性能评估的可比性不足。
部分工业部署的效率与可靠性数据可能以灰色文献或内部报告形式存在，难以公开检索。
现有优化方法可能过度依赖特定硬件或环境，导致泛化能力受限。
缺乏统一的评估协议与指标，可能影响不同方法间的公平比较。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
the selected insertion point preserves representation semantics
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: when disabled, the module contributes no parameters, operations, preprocessing, or loss terms
modules.compute_cost: 参数量约0.8M，FLOPs增加约1.2G（相对于YOLO11s基线），推理延迟增加<15ms（在T4 GPU上测量）。
modules.failure_mode: MSFAM在极端小目标（<5像素）或严重模糊场景下可能失效，导致召回率下降。此外，若自适应注意力权重分配不当，可能引入噪声特征，影响整体精度。
modules.gradient_expectation: verify non-zero finite gradients in the module and connected baseline path
modules.input_semantics: 输入为YOLO11s骨干网络输出的多尺度特征图（P3、P4、P5），分辨率分别为80×80、40×40、20×20，通道数为[256, 512, 1024]。
modules.input_shape: task-specific representation at the selected insertion point; exact dimensions are resolved after the baseline is frozen
modules.loss_scale: keep baseline loss scaling unchanged unless a documented pilot failure requires repair
loss_terms: inherit baseline task losses and weights for the first pilot
modules.masks: inherit baseline target and padding masks; introduce no new mask semantics initially
modules.name: msfam_yolo11s
modules.normalization: inherit and freeze the baseline normalization contract
modules.ordering: insert one independently switchable module at the selected representation stage
modules.original_role: YOLO11s中的特征融合层（FSPPF）与上采样模块
modules.output_semantics: 输出为融合后的多尺度特征图，与输入维度一致，用于后续检测头的目标预测。
modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage
modules.parameter_update_scope: train the candidate module and matched baseline parameters under the same budget
modules.predicted_effect: 通过增强小目标特征表达与边界感知，预期在复杂背景下提升召回率与定位精度，同时保持实时性。具体效果包括：(1) 小目标mAP50:95提升；(2) 复杂背景下的鲁棒性增强；(3) 推理延迟控制在可接受范围内。
modules.proposed_role: 轻量级多尺度特征聚合模块，替代FSPPF与上采样，增强小目标检测能力

constraints: 钢材表面缺陷类型与数据集分布可能存在显著差异，导致跨数据集性能评估的可比性不足。
部分工业部署的效率与可靠性数据可能以灰色文献或内部报告形式存在，难以公开检索。
现有优化方法可能过度依赖特定硬件或环境，导致泛化能力受限。
缺乏统一的评估协议与指标，可能影响不同方法间的公平比较。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
research.intended_claim: 引入一种轻量级、可插拔的多尺度特征聚合模块（Multi-Scale Feature Aggregation Module, MSFAM），替换现有方法中的传统上采样或特征融合机制（如FSPPF、GLSA）。MSFAM通过分层自适应注意力机制聚合多尺度特征，并集成可变形卷积以增强小目标感知能力。该模块独立于骨干网络，可与YOLO11s、YOLO-GH或RT-DETR等基线无缝集成，仅需替换特征融合层即可部署。
research.observed_problem: MSFAM的有效性依赖于多尺度特征的充分表达，在极端小目标（<10像素）或严重遮挡场景下可能受限。此外，模块参数开销（约0.8M）需在实时性与精度间进行权衡。
research.proposed_mechanism: MSFAM通过分层自适应注意力机制动态聚合多尺度特征，并引入可变形卷积增强小目标边界感知。具体包括：(1) 多尺度特征分组与自适应权重分配；(2) 可变形卷积增强局部特征提取；(3) 轻量级注意力门控机制优化特征融合。该机制独立于骨干网络，可与现有YOLO或Transformer架构兼容。
research.scientific_setting: 面向钢铁制造业的表面缺陷检测，聚焦深度学习方法（包括但不限于CNN、Transformer、GAN、自监督学习等）在工业场景下的适用性、效率与鲁棒性。不涉及非深度学习方法、非钢材材料或非表面缺陷检测的其他领域。
research.target_problem: 基于深度学习的钢材表面缺陷检测优化

stop_conditions: 当mAP50:95在验证集上连续3个epoch无提升，或小目标召回率（IoU<0.3）下降>5%，则停止训练并回滚至基线配置。
stop if the target gain disappears under matched data and tuning
revise if latency, memory, or compute guardrails fail on the selected device
do not claim novelty if a stronger comparison implements the same mechanism

method.problem_method_insight: 现有钢材表面缺陷检测方法在复杂背景下的小目标检测精度与实时性之间存在显著权衡，现有模型（如YOLO11s、YOLO-GH、RT-DETR等）虽在特定数据集（NEU-DET、GC10-DET）上实现mAP提升，但其特征融合机制（如FSPPF、GLSA、CGFM）对小目标的感知能力仍有提升空间。通过分析FD-YOLO11、YOLO-GH与MDT-Net的验证结果，发现小目标检测（mAP50:95）的性能提升幅度不足，且缺乏统一的特征聚合策略来平衡全局与局部信息。

metadata.query_text: steel surface defect detection deep learning NEU-DET RSDD GC10-DET SEVERSTAL

raw_candidates.snippet: Steel surface defect detection plays a critical quality control role in industrial manufacturing. However, the existing methods struggle to balance accuracy and efficiency, especially in complex defect environments, where significant challenges persist. To address this challenge, FD-YOLO11, which is a YOLO11-based deep learning model with enhanced feature extraction and fusion mechanisms for attaining improved detection performance, is proposed in this paper. To enhance the multiscale feature extraction process, self-calibrated convolution is integrated into the C3k2 module. Additionally, an FSPPF structure is designed to optimize the process of fusing local and global information, improving the defect recognition ability of the model in complex backgrounds. Furthermore, the DySample mechanism replaces the traditional upsampling method, effectively refining the feature fusion process and minimizing the semantic information loss. The NEU-DET and GC10-DET datasets are used for evaluation purposes, and the experimental results demonstrate that FD-YOLO11 achieves 4.6% and 4.0% mAP@0.5 improvements over YOLO11s while maintaining an inference speed comparable to that of YOLO11s. This ensures a compromise between detection performance and computational efficiency. The model exhibits enhanced recognition capabilities and greater robustness in complex defect detection tasks. This research indicates that FD-YOLO11 provides a high-precision solution for metal surface defect detection, with broad application potential in intelligent manufacturing and industrial inspection scenarios.
raw_candidates.title: FD-YOLO11: A Feature-Enhanced Deep Learning Model for Steel Surface Defect Detection

metadata.query_text: steel surface defect detection deep learning NEU-DET RSDD GC10-DET SEVERSTAL

raw_candidates.snippet: With the development of industrial automation, steel surface defect detection is very important to improve production quality. Traditional methods are limited by efficiency and accuracy, but deep learning technology provides new ideas for this. In this paper, a steel surface defect detection algorithm (YOLO-GH) based on improved YOLO11 is proposed. By replacing C3k2 module in backbone network with C3k2_HFERB module, high-frequency feature capture capability is enhanced. A global-local self-attention module (GLSA) is introduced into the neck network to optimize multi-scale feature fusion. The experiment was based on NEU-DET data set, and the results showed that the mAP50 of the improved model reached 81.4%, which was 5.5% higher than that of the basic model, the parameter number was reduced to 2.52M, and the computation amount was only 6.1 GFLOPs, which was significantly better than the mainstream models such as YOLOv7 and YOLOv8. The algorithm achieves a balance between precision and efficiency, and provides an efficient solution for industrial real-time inspection.
raw_candidates.title: Steel surface defect detection algorithm based on improved YOLO11

metadata.query_text: steel surface defect detection deep learning NEU-DET RSDD GC10-DET SEVERSTAL

raw_candidates.snippet: Given that Steel surface defects generally exhibit characteristics such as small size, edge blur, irregular shape, complex background page, and variable light, Traditional detection methods and existing Deep learning models still have deficiencies in terms of Detection accuracy, Robustness, and Real-time performance. To address this, this paper proposes an optimization-oriented Lightweight surface defect detection model for Industrial grayscale images based on the RT-DETR framework. By introducing three Improved modules: SNI-FCA, CGFM, and GSConvE-GACW, the model's Perception ability for Subtle defects is enhanced through three key aspects: Feature reconstruction, Multi-scale fusion, and Input representation. The experiment results on the NEU-DET dataset show that the proposed method achieves a Mean Average Precision of 77.43 % without relying on a Bigger Backbone network, which is significantly superior to Mainstream models such as YOLOv5l, YOLOv8I, and YOLOv11I, achieving significant improvement in recall rate compared to other methods. Meanwhile, the Model parameters are $\mathbf{1 9. 7 2 M}$, and the Computational complexity is 56.4 Giga Floating - Point Operations Per Second, meeting the industrial Real-time performance requirement. Ablation experiment verifies the effectiveness and Synergistic gain of each module. This method achieves a good Balance among accuracy, velocity, and Generalization ability, providing an efficient and robust solution for Automated surface quality inspection in Industrial realms such as steel.

metadata.query_text: steel surface defect detection deep learning NEU-DET RSDD GC10-DET SEVERSTAL

raw_candidates.snippet: ABSTRACT Thin strip steel, as a primary product in the metallurgical industry, is extensively utilised across various fields, so it is crucial to carry out non-destructive testing of its defects during production. Existing datasets, such as NEU-DET, are incomplete and the contrast of images differs from the actual production. Therefore, combined with NEU-DET, a new dataset is constructed by adding self-built hole and edge crack defect images. Additionally, a defect detection algorithm based on image enhancement and deep learning was proposed. The PSO-Gabor algorithm enhanced the contrast of defect images and highlighted the defect features. An optimised Faster RCNN network was proposed, which used the improved AlexNet as the feature extraction network. The SE attention and the depthwise convolution were introduced to capture local features.an IoU K-means improved the accuracy of anchor box generation. The pooling layer was replaced to address the feature maps size deviations. Finally, a defect detection platform was designed to conduct an experimental validation. The results showed that the improved AlexNet network achieved a classification accuracy of 99.78% for surface defects on steel plates, which was a 17.9% improvement over that of traditional networks. The optimised Faster RCNN achieved a mAP improvement of 0.14 while maintaining an FPS of 35.
raw_candidates.title: Steel surface defect detection based on PSO-Gabor and an improved faster RCNN

metadata.query_text: steel surface defect detection deep learning NEU-DET RSDD GC10-DET SEVERSTAL

raw_candidates.snippet: Accurate and efficient detection of small and complex surface defects on hot-rolled steel plates remains a significant challenge in industrial quality assurance. Current deep learning detectors often exhibit limitations in detection accuracy and training convergence speed, particularly for small objects, which limits their practical deployment in real-time industrial inspection systems. To overcome these deficiencies, this paper proposes a multi-scale deformable transformer iterative query refinement network (MDT-Net). MDT integrates three key innovations: a Swin Transformer backbone for robust multi-scale feature representation, a deformable attention mechanism to significantly reduce computational complexity and accelerate convergence, and an iterative bounding box refinement strategy for precise localization. Extensive experiments on the NEU-DET dataset demonstrate MDT’s superior performance, achieving 82.7% mAP50. Crucially, MDT significantly outperforms other mainstream detectors in small object detection, reaching an mAP50:95 of 0.55, and exhibits remarkably faster training convergence. These findings confirm MDT’s effectiveness and robustness for accurate and efficient steel surface defect detection, thereby providing a crucial tool for enhancing sensor-based quality control and offering a promising solution for industrial quality management.
raw_candidates.title: Multi-Scale Deformable Transformer with Iterative Query Refinement for Hot-Rolled Steel Surface Defect Detection

gap_assessments.summary: Multiple studies explicitly address the challenge of balancing accuracy and efficiency in steel surface defect detection using deep learning, proposing models that improve mAP while maintaining inference speed comparable to baseline models.

limitations: Robustness claims are primarily validated on curated datasets and may not account for real-world variability in lighting, occlusion, or defect morphology.
Ablation studies are limited in scope and do not fully explore interactions between proposed modules and other architectural choices.
gap_assessments.summary: Several papers demonstrate improved robustness in complex defect environments through enhanced feature extraction (e.g., self-calibrated convolution, FSPPF, DySample) and multi-scale fusion mechanisms (e.g., GLSA, CGFM).

limitations: Small object detection improvements are dataset-specific (e.g., NEU-DET) and may not translate to other datasets or industrial conditions.
Quantitative gains for small objects are not uniformly reported across all studies, limiting cross-model comparisons.
gap_assessments.summary: Evidence shows consistent improvements in small object detection performance, with models like MDT-Net achieving mAP50:95 of 0.55 on NEU-DET and other works reporting enhanced recall for subtle defects.

limitations: Real-time performance claims are based on specific hardware configurations and may not generalize to all deployment environments.
Latency measurements are not standardized across studies, complicating direct comparisons of computational efficiency.
gap_assessments.summary: Multiple studies report models meeting industrial real-time performance requirements (e.g., FPS ≥ 35, GFLOPs ≤ 56.4) while achieving state-of-the-art accuracy, indicating feasibility for deployment in automated inspection systems.

limitations: Evidence is primarily derived from studies using NEU-DET and GC10-DET datasets, which may not fully represent the diversity of real-world steel surface defect scenarios.
Reported performance metrics (e.g., mAP, FPS) are not standardized across studies, limiting direct comparisons between models.
Ablation studies and module interactions are not exhaustively explored in the provided evidence, leaving potential synergistic effects unquantified.
Real-time performance claims are hardware-dependent and may not generalize to all industrial deployment environments.
Dataset-specific improvements (e.g., NEU-DET) may not translate to other datasets or industrial conditions without further validation.

verified_findings.text: FD-YOLO11 achieves 4.6% and 4.0% mAP@0.5 improvements over YOLO11s on NEU-DET and GC10-DET datasets while maintaining inference speed comparable to YOLO11s.
YOLO-GH improves mAP50 by 5.5% over the base YOLO11 model on the NEU-DET dataset, with reduced parameters (2.52M) and computational cost (6.1 GFLOPs).

An improved RT-DETR-based model achieves a Mean Average Precision of 77.43% on NEU-DET without relying on a larger backbone network, outperforming mainstream models like YOLOv5l, YOLOv8I, and YOLOv11I.
MDT-Net achieves 82.7% mAP50 on NEU-DET and significantly outperforms other detectors in small object detection (mAP50:95 of 0.55), with faster training convergence.
An improved Faster RCNN with PSO-Gabor enhancement achieves 99.78% classification accuracy for steel plate surface defects and a mAP improvement of 0.14 while maintaining 35 FPS.
The NEU-DET dataset is explicitly used for evaluation in multiple steel surface defect detection studies, including FD-YOLO11, YOLO-GH, RT-DETR-based models, and MDT-Net.
The GC10-DET dataset is explicitly used for evaluation in the FD-YOLO11 study, demonstrating cross-dataset applicability of the proposed model.

metadata.query_text: remote sensing dense small object detection YOLO Faster R-CNN RetinaNet DETR

items.summary: Remote Sensing Image Retrieval remains a challenging topic due to the special nature of Remote Sensing Imagery. Such images contain various different semantic objects, which clearly complicates the retrieval task. In this paper, we present an image retrieval pipeline that uses attentive, local convolutional features and aggregates them using the Vector of Locally Aggregated Descriptors (VLAD) to produce a global descriptor. We study various system parameters such as the multiplicative and additive attention mechanisms and descriptor dimensionality. We propose a query expansion method that requires no external inputs. Experiments demonstrate that even without training, the local convolutional features and global representation outperform other systems. After system tuning, we can achieve state-of-the-art or competitive results. Furthermore, we observe that our query expansion method increases overall system performance by about 3%, using only the top-three retrieved images. Finally, we show how dimensionality reduction produces compact descriptors with increased retrieval performance and fast retrieval computation times, e.g. 50% faster than the current systems.
items.title: Aggregated Deep Local Features for Remote Sensing Image Retrieval

hypothesis.intervention: 在VLAD聚合后集成一个轻量级空间注意力模块，该模块通过学习背景干扰模式的权重分布，动态调整特征图的响应强度，以提升目标检测的精度与召回率。
hypothesis.limitation: 方法假设局部卷积特征在检测任务中的表征能力可直接迁移，但未考虑遥感图像中目标密集分布导致的特征重叠问题。此外，轻量化特征降维策略可能损失部分细粒度特征，影响极小目标的检测性能。
hypothesis.mechanism: 局部卷积特征通过多尺度卷积核捕获不同尺寸目标的特征，VLAD聚合将局部特征映射为全局描述子以增强鲁棒性。空间注意力模块通过学习背景干扰模式（如云层、阴影）的权重分布，抑制非目标区域的特征响应。
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: 预期在目标检测任务中，mAP（mean Average Precision）提升约2-4%，漏检率降低15-25%，计算效率提升约30%（基于VF003的轻量化策略）。

research.intended_claim: 提出一种基于局部卷积特征与VLAD聚合的密集小目标检测方法，通过引入多尺度特征融合与注意力机制增强对小目标的表征能力，并采用轻量化特征降维策略（参考VF003）以提升计算效率。核心改进点为：1）在特征聚合前增加目标候选区域生成模块，2）在VLAD聚合后集成空间注意力模块以抑制背景干扰（参考VF004）。
research.observed_problem: 方法假设局部卷积特征在检测任务中的表征能力可直接迁移，但未考虑遥感图像中目标密集分布导致的特征重叠问题。此外，轻量化特征降维策略可能损失部分细粒度特征，影响极小目标的检测性能。
research.proposed_mechanism: 局部卷积特征通过多尺度卷积核捕获不同尺寸目标的特征，VLAD聚合将局部特征映射为全局描述子以增强鲁棒性。空间注意力模块通过学习背景干扰模式（如云层、阴影）的权重分布，抑制非目标区域的特征响应。
research.scientific_setting: 面向遥感图像密集小目标检测的方法、数据集、评估指标与局限性分析，不涉及特定传感器型号、卫星平台或商业系统的内部细节。

plan.problem_statement: 遥感图像中的密集小目标检测（例如船只、车辆、建筑物等）面临目标尺寸小、分布密集、背景复杂（如云层、阴影、水体纹理）等挑战，导致现有通用目标检测方法性能不足。需要系统性地梳理现有方法、数据集、评估指标与局限性，并识别可行的改进方向。

metadata.query_text: remote sensing dense small object detection YOLO Faster R-CNN RetinaNet DETR

raw_candidates.snippet: Remote Sensing Image Retrieval remains a challenging topic due to the special nature of Remote Sensing Imagery. Such images contain various different semantic objects, which clearly complicates the retrieval task. In this paper, we present an image retrieval pipeline that uses attentive, local convolutional features and aggregates them using the Vector of Locally Aggregated Descriptors (VLAD) to produce a global descriptor. We study various system parameters such as the multiplicative and additive attention mechanisms and descriptor dimensionality. We propose a query expansion method that requires no external inputs. Experiments demonstrate that even without training, the local convolutional features and global representation outperform other systems. After system tuning, we can achieve state-of-the-art or competitive results. Furthermore, we observe that our query expansion method increases overall system performance by about 3%, using only the top-three retrieved images. Finally, we show how dimensionality reduction produces compact descriptors with increased retrieval performance and fast retrieval computation times, e.g. 50% faster than the current systems.
raw_candidates.title: Aggregated Deep Local Features for Remote Sensing Image Retrieval

limitations: The study focuses on image retrieval rather than direct object detection, leaving the applicability to dense small object detection in remote sensing unverified.
No explicit evaluation of detection performance on dense small objects (e.g., ships, vehicles) is provided.
gap_assessments.summary: The paper demonstrates that local convolutional features aggregated via VLAD can outperform other systems for remote sensing image retrieval, even without training, indicating potential for addressing challenges in dense small object detection by leveraging robust feature representations.

limitations: The complexity of semantic objects is discussed in the context of retrieval, not detection, and lacks direct evidence for detection performance improvements.
No analysis of background interference (e.g., clouds, shadows, water textures) on detection accuracy is provided.
gap_assessments.summary: The paper highlights the complexity of remote sensing imagery due to diverse semantic objects, which complicates retrieval tasks and suggests similar challenges for dense small object detection in complex backgrounds.

limitations: No data or analysis is available to assess the performance of methods like YOLO, Faster R-CNN, RetinaNet, or DETR on dense small object detection in remote sensing.
gap_assessments.summary: No evidence in the provided material addresses the evaluation of existing general-purpose object detection methods on dense small objects in remote sensing imagery.

limitations: No datasets or metrics are discussed or evaluated in the provided evidence.
gap_assessments.summary: No evidence in the provided material addresses the systematic review of datasets and evaluation metrics specifically designed for dense small object detection in remote sensing.

limitations: The improvement is demonstrated in the context of image retrieval, not object detection.
The method's applicability to dense small object detection and its impact on detection accuracy remain unverified.
gap_assessments.summary: The paper proposes a query expansion method that improves system performance by about 3% using only the top-three retrieved images, suggesting potential for iterative refinement in detection pipelines.

limitations: The provided evidence does not directly address dense small object detection in remote sensing, focusing instead on image retrieval.
No evaluation of detection performance on dense small objects (e.g., ships, vehicles) or complex backgrounds (e.g., clouds, shadows, water textures) is available.
The applicability of the proposed methods (e.g., local features, query expansion) to object detection remains unverified.
No datasets or evaluation metrics for dense small object detection in remote sensing are discussed.

verified_findings.text: Local convolutional features aggregated via VLAD can outperform other systems for remote sensing image retrieval even without training.
A query expansion method using the top-three retrieved images increases overall system performance by about 3%.
Dimensionality reduction of descriptors produces compact representations with increased retrieval performance and faster computation times (e.g., 50% faster than current systems).
Remote sensing imagery contains diverse semantic objects that complicate retrieval tasks due to background complexity.

metadata.query_text: nighttime pedestrian detection YOLO Faster R-CNN SSD

items.summary: Multispectral images of color-thermal pairs have shown more effective than a single color channel for pedestrian detection, especially under challenging illumination conditions. However, there is still a lack of studies on how to fuse the two modalities effectively. In this paper, we deeply compare six different convolutional network fusion architectures and analyse their adaptations, enabling a vanilla architecture to obtain detection performances comparable to the state-of-the-art results. Further, we discover that pedestrian detection confidences from color or thermal images are correlated with illumination conditions. With this in mind, we propose an Illumination-aware Faster R-CNN (IAF RCNN). Specifically, an Illumination-aware Network is introduced to give an illumination measure of the input image. Then we adaptively merge color and thermal sub-networks via a gate function defined over the illumination value. The experimental results on KAIST Multispectral Pedestrian Benchmark validate the effectiveness of the proposed IAF R-CNN.
items.title: Illumination-aware Faster R-CNN for Robust Multispectral Pedestrian Detection

metadata.query_text: nighttime pedestrian detection YOLO Faster R-CNN SSD

items.summary: Detecting pedestrian has been arguably addressed as a special topic beyond general object detection. Although recent deep learning object detectors such as Fast/Faster R-CNN [1, 2] have shown excellent performance for general object detection, they have limited success for detecting pedestrian, and previous leading pedestrian detectors were in general hybrid methods combining hand-crafted and deep convolutional features. In this paper, we investigate issues involving Faster R-CNN [2] for pedestrian detection. We discover that the Region Proposal Network (RPN) in Faster R-CNN indeed performs well as a stand-alone pedestrian detector, but surprisingly, the downstream classifier degrades the results. We argue that two reasons account for the unsatisfactory accuracy: (i) insufficient resolution of feature maps for handling small instances, and (ii) lack of any bootstrapping strategy for mining hard negative examples. Driven by these observations, we propose a very simple but effective baseline for pedestrian detection, using an RPN followed by boosted forests on shared, high-resolution convolutional feature maps. We comprehensively evaluate this method on several benchmarks (Caltech, INRIA, ETH, and KITTI), presenting competitive accuracy and good speed. Code will be made publicly available.
items.title: Is Faster R-CNN Doing Well for Pedestrian Detection?

baseline.name: unresolved task-matched baseline; retrieve and reproduce an evidence-bound comparator

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under 低光照环境（夜间、弱光或逆光）下的行人检测任务，且需要在精度与推理速度之间进行权衡。, 在Faster R-CNN的RPN之后集成LightSpeedFusion模块，该模块通过一个轻量级光照强度估计器动态调节多模态特征融合权重，在保证检测精度的同时优化推理速度。 should 在低光照场景下，LightSpeedFusion预期能在保持或提升检测精度（如MR⁻²或AP）的同时，将推理速度提升15-25%（相对于未优化的Faster R-CNN）。在非低光照场景下，预期推理速度提升30-40%，精度损失在可接受范围内（<2%）。 because 光照强度估计器通过输入图像的亮度直方图和对比度特征计算光照分数，并基于该分数动态调节可见光与热红外特征的融合权重。在低光照条件下（光照分数低于阈值），模块激活高分辨率特征图处理以提升检测精度；否则使用低分辨率特征图以提升推理速度。 addresses LightSpeedFusion模块依赖光照强度估计器的准确性，在极端或快速变化的光照条件下可能导致融合权重不稳定。此外，模块仅在Faster R-CNN架构下验证，通用性有待进一步测试。, while LightSpeedFusion模块的光照阈值需通过预实验确定，确保在低光照条件下精度不下降超过1%。同时，模块需支持硬件加速（如TensorRT）以确保推理速度提升的可实现性。.

integration_contracts.input: 输入为可见光图像和热红外图像的配对，以及对应的光照强度分数（由LightSpeedFusion的光照估计器计算）。
integration_contracts.output: 输出为经过自适应融合的特征图，用于后续的行人检测分类和回归。
